artPrompt: A grinning goblin rogue in a too-large pinstripe coat, one clawed hand clapped warmly on an unseen shoulder, the other already slipping behind his back, beady eyes glinting with practiced sincerity. Cigar smoke curls through a single pool of amber lamplight that catches his teeth while the rain-slick back room dissolves into ink-black shadow. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: A Goblin You Can Trust
text:
When A Goblin You Can Trust enters the battlefield, point at one opponent and say "this one's a friend of mine." That player draws a card and you create a Favor token.
When A Goblin You Can Trust dies, the player you pointed at sacrifices a creature. Turns out you couldn't, actually. Trust him, that is.
flavorText: "Stand-up guy. Solid. Loyal as the day is long." The day was short. It was December.
